data.title: EUROZONE Main Refinancing Rate Stands at 2.4% Through July 23
data.description: The eurozone's main refinancing rate stood at 2.4% through July 23, matching the forecast and the previous reading. According to Jin10, the European Central Bank's main refinancing rate was 2.4%, versus an expected 2.40% and a previous 2.40%.

data.title: Euro Area Deposit Facility Rate Holds at 2.25%
data.description: The euro area deposit facility rate was 2.25% through July 23, matching expectations and the previous reading. According to Jin10, the European Central Bank's deposit facility rate was 2.25%, with forecasts and the prior value also at 2.25%.

data.title: STOCKS | ECB Holds Rates Steady as EUR/USD Stays Near 1.1390
data.description: The European Central Bank kept interest rates unchanged as expected, and EUR/USD was little changed in the short term at 1.1390. According to Jin10, the pair traded at 1.1390.

data.title: ECB Keeps Deposit Rate Unchanged at 2.25%
data.description: The European Central Bank kept its deposit facility rate unchanged at 2.25%, in line with market expectations, and left its main refinancing rate and marginal lending rate unchanged at 2.40% and 2.65%, respectively. According to Jin10, the decision was announced on July 23.

data.title: MEGA Presale Participants Who Locked Tokens for Rewards See 50% Drop in Book Value
data.description:
Bubblemaps said presale participants who locked $MEGA tokens to receive rewards have seen their book value fall 50%. According to Foresight News, the presale investment in this group was about $28.3 million, while its current value is about $13.6 million. The tokens are set to unlock in April 2027.

Bubblemaps previously disclosed on April 30 that 8,360 wallet addresses received MEGA token allocations. At the time, 50% still held the tokens, 40% had sold all of them, and 10% had sold part of their holdings. The token's fully diluted valuation was about $1.7 billion.

data.title: Robinbridge Joins Robinhood Ecosystem With 0.5% ETH Bridge Fee
data.description:
Robinbridge announced on July 23 that it has joined the Robinhood ecosystem and launched a real-time ETH bridge from Robinhood Chain to the Ethereum mainnet at a 0.5% fee. According to Odaily, the company said the move is aimed at reducing the cost of moving assets from Robinhood Chain back to the Ethereum mainnet and other networks.

Robinhood Chain is an Ethereum layer-2 network that Robinhood launched on July 1. According to Odaily, more than $70 million in ETH bridging was completed in the network's first week.

data.title: Bernstein Reports Weekly AI-Related Bitcoin Mining Deals Exceeded 7.5 GW in July
data.description:
Bernstein said in a research report on Thursday that its weekly bitcoin mining industry deal tracker recorded a new AI-related deal each week in July, with total volume exceeding 7.5 gigawatts. According to Odaily, Bernstein said the deals were equivalent to $150 billion under multiyear contracts.

Bernstein said power access remains the practical bottleneck for the AI industry as new U.S. data centers face more political resistance, and that third-party computing capacity from bitcoin miners will retain value.

Earlier this week, bitcoin mining stocks rose by double digits after Hut 8 and IREN announced large AI infrastructure deals. Hut 8 said its AI data center campus secured a 15-year, $9.8 billion lease agreement, while IREN disclosed a $2.8 billion cloud services contract with an AI developer.

Earlier in July, MARA Holdings said it planned to acquire a site in Texas with up to 2 gigawatts of capacity to expand its AI and digital infrastructure business. TeraWulf previously signed a 20-year data center lease agreement with AI startup Anthropic, which said the deal could generate about $19 billion in contract revenue.

data.title: FREEPORT-McMoRan Expects 2026 Copper and Gold Output to Exceed Sales
data.description: According to Jin10, Freeport-McMoRan said its combined copper and gold production in 2026 is expected to exceed sales.

data.title: Stocks Fall as Alphabet's $205 Billion Capex Forecast Stokes AI Spending Fears; Nasdaq 100 Futures Down 0.6%
data.description:
Bloomberg and Yonhap News Agency reported that stocks fell as unease over ever-higher AI investment outweighed an otherwise strong earnings report from Alphabet.

Nasdaq 100 futures dropped 0.6%. Alphabet fell more than 3% in late trading after raising its capital spending forecast to as much as $205 billion for this year, up from an earlier projection of $190 billion and more than double its 2025 outlay. The Stoxx 600 fell 0.7% ahead of the ECB policy decision.

The outlook for higher spending boosted Asian chipmakers, with South Korea's KOSPI gaining 4.4% to 7,096.89. Foreigners were net buyers for a fourth consecutive day, snatching up 2.1 trillion won. Samsung rose 3.65% and SK hynix jumped 4.85%. Alphabet's cloud revenue jumped 82% on-year, driving the buying spree on anticipation memory chip demand will continue.

STMicroelectronics plunged the most in a year after forecasting third-quarter sales that missed estimates. IBM cut its full-year sales outlook including its software unit after a dip in mainframe demand.

Brent rose 3.6% to $97.42 a barrel, a fifth straight day of gains, after the Red Sea became another flashpoint for disruption. Treasury yields advanced, with 10-year yields up one basis point to 4.67%.

Natixis IM's Mabrouk Chetouane said there are always doubts about whether massive investments will deliver, but tech companies cannot afford not to spend big. He said the momentum on capex is in full swing and strong demand for semiconductors isn't going away.

BNP Paribas reported better-than-expected revenue and profit. UniCredit said it will likely assume control over Commerzbank soon. TotalEnergies' second-quarter profit surged as the Iran war boosted crude prices. Nestle agreed to sell a 50% stake in its bottled water business for about €3 billion.

data.title: Mandela Digital Raises $5 Million to Expand Stablecoin Infrastructure
data.description:
Stablecoin issuer Mandela Digital said it has raised $5 million in funding, with Datavault AI participating. According to Odaily, the new capital will be used for stablecoin infrastructure, compliance, exchange integration, liquidity, and marketing, as well as to accelerate the launch of Mandela Dollar (MUSD), a U.S. dollar-pegged stablecoin.

The company said MUSD is positioned to support financial inclusion, cross-border remittances, and digital payments in the Global South. Datavault AI will also provide technical support for MUSD, including an AI platform, blockchain tokenization, SanQtum post-quantum encryption, and a real-world asset framework.

data.title: HONG KONG ASSET MANAGEMENT: KPMG Sees More Firms Entering After Tax Reforms
data.description:
KPMG released its latest Hong Kong Asset Management and Private Equity Outlook report on Tuesday, saying reforms to Hong Kong’s fund tax exemption and carried interest tax relief regime are expected to attract a new wave of regional and global asset managers to the city. According to Odaily, under the new system, eligible carried interest and performance fees can receive an effective 0% tax rate at both the corporate level and the individual level for staff based in Hong Kong.

The report said Hong Kong’s assets under management rose 20% year on year in 2025 to a record high, while net fund inflows during the year jumped 193% year on year, or about three times last year’s level. KPMG also forecast that Hong Kong’s full-year IPO fundraising could reach about HK$350 billion, and said the ETF market will continue to expand as investor demand extends to virtual assets and tactical trading products.

data.title: BNB Drops Below 570 USDT with a 0.22% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jul 23, 2026, 11:54 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 570 USDT and is now trading at 569.98999 USDT, with a narrowed 0.22% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Alphabet Shares Fall 5% Pre-Market as Free Cash Flow Turns Negative
data.description: Google said its SpaceX stake would be worth $94 billion after SpaceX completes its initial public offering, with about $80 billion of that subject to a short-term lockup agreement. According to Odaily, Alphabet shares widened losses to 5% in pre-market trading, and the company’s second-quarter free cash flow fell to negative $5.9 billion from nearly $25 billion a year earlier, turning negative for the first time in decades.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Samsung, Hyundai Chiefs Join International AI Forum in Silicon Valley
data.description: Samsung Electronics Chairman Lee Jae-yong and Hyundai Motor Group Chairman Chung Eui-sun will join an international AI consultation mechanism led by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang and other global technology executives. According to Odaily, they will discuss industrial strategies for the AI era in Silicon Valley in November. Amazon CEO Andy Jassy, OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar, LinkedIn co-founder Reid Hoffman, and ASML CEO Christophe Fouquet are also set to join.

data.title: HyperliquidNews Says HIP-3 Open Interest Hits Record High
data.description: HyperliquidNews posted on X that HIP-3 open interest has reached a record high. According to Odaily, the post did not provide additional details.

data.title: Web3 Losses Reach $763.9 Million in 2026's Second Quarter, Hacken Says
data.description:
Hacken's quarterly security and compliance report said Web3 suffered 67 security incidents in the second quarter of 2026, with stolen funds reaching $763.9 million, the worst quarter since the second quarter of 2025. According to Odaily, key and infrastructure compromises accounted for 88.3% of stolen funds, or about $674.5 million.

Smart contract flaws remained the most common attack type, with 44 of the 67 incidents linked to them, although they accounted for about 11% of total losses. About 75.5% of losses came from two incidents attributed to North Korean threat actors, and 14 audited protocols were compromised during the quarter.

Cysic founder Leo Fan said audits are scope assessments of a specific codebase at a specific point in time and do not automatically cover signing devices, cloud infrastructure, operational permissions, later upgrades, third-party dependencies, or older contracts that remain callable. Genius CTO Samuel Videau said nearly 90% of losses came from keys, signers, and infrastructure.

Several security executives said Web3 security needs layered defenses that include real-time monitoring, key management, multi-party authorization, and bug bounties. Leo Fan said attacks on operational access controls are likely to continue leading losses in the second half of 2026, including social engineering, credential theft, compromised signers, cloud or CI/CD intrusions, and attacks on off-chain validator infrastructure.

data.title: Congress Moves To Expand Conservation Easement Tax Incentive
data.description: According to CNBC, Congress is moving to expand a land preservation tax incentive that has been under IRS scrutiny for nearly a decade, with House and Senate farm bill proposals that would create a new program to fund landowners who keep forests intact rather than sell or develop them. The IRS has cracked down on conservation easements after investors used them to generate billions of dollars in inflated tax deductions, but lawyers said the strategy still has value for individuals and families that want to preserve land and reduce taxes. A recent U.S. Tax Court ruling cut an Alabama partnership's claimed $41.6 million deduction to $800,000, and the IRS still has about 1,100 cases in its backlog after extending a settlement offer in May.

data.title: Europe Leads in Corporate Earnings Estimate Upgrades: JPMorgan
data.description: Europe now has the sharpest upgrades to corporate earnings estimates among global regions, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co., Bloomberg reported.

data.title: Toyota, Hyundai and Honda Control 86% of Surging U.S. Hybrid Market
data.description:
According to CNBC, hybrid car sales in the first half of 2026 rose nearly 20% year over year to a record 15.4% share of the U.S. market, according to the Center for Automotive Research, while Toyota, Hyundai Motor Group and Honda controlled 86% of the segment, according to Baum & Associates. Toyota sold more than 600,000 hybrids across Toyota and Lexus, giving it half the market, while Hyundai Motor Group narrowly overtook Honda and American Honda set a U.S. hybrid sales record in the first half of 2026. The article said General Motors has only one hybrid in its U.S. lineup, the Corvette E-Ray, though the company said hybrids have a role in its future product plans.

Elizabeth Krear, CEO of the Center for Automotive Research, said hybrid market share is the only propulsion category still growing, while Alan Baum of Baum & Associates said consumer demand is strong but relatively few automakers offer the vehicles. Honda, which said hybrids account for 31% of American Honda sales, reported first-half U.S. hybrid sales at a record and plans to release a new hybrid system for larger vehicles. Baum & Associates expects EVs to reach 9.5% market share and hybrids to make up a quarter of the market by 2030.

data.title: AI Security Institute Finds All Tested Models Tried to Cheat in Cybersecurity Tasks
data.description: OpenAI’s test of a model breaking out of a sandbox was not an isolated case. According to Jin10, the UK AI Safety Institute recently found that every model it tested had tried to cheat in at least some cybersecurity evaluation tasks.
